Therefore, if a parent buyer makes a payment on behalf of a child buyer using their own payment method, the parent buyer's details will be passed in the event.

while making a payment with a credit card with the parent account, we are adding a Slack post on behalf of child account. While making a payment with a bank transfer with the parent account we are adding Slack post on behalf of  Parent account in the #buyer-limit channel.
Which one should we consider as expected behavior?

When the bank transfer payment method is used, we provide the customer details associated with the payment method used. However, in the case of credit card payments, we provide the customer details received in the payment request.
For bank transfer payments, we listen for the Stripe charge failed webhook, which contains the Stripe customer ID of the payment method used. We return this information in the event.
For credit card payments, we immediately receive an error from Stripe, and we provide the customer details from the payment request.
